claim: OCRmyPDF adds a text layer to scanned PDFs, preserves image content, supports languages, rotate-pages, deskew, multiprocessing, and uses Tesseract.
implication: Use OCRmyPDF as the first local OCR sidecar rather than reimplementing OCR/PDF assembly in Rust.

claim: OCRmyPDF can emit a sidecar text file and notes that sidecar text only includes OCR-performed pages; it also documents Tesseract direct image-to-PDF output but says OCRmyPDF adds PDF/image-processing features Tesseract alone lacks.
implication: For `cnv`, the derived searchable PDF is the primary artifact; sidecar text is useful for diagnostics, not a replacement for the normal pipeline.

claim: Tesseract can create searchable PDFs and hOCR output directly from images.
implication: Tesseract direct integration is viable only after `cnv` owns rasterization/page assembly; defer behind OCRmyPDF.

claim: Docling warns that enabling OCR, table structure, and formulas increases processing time and requires system OCR engine installation when OCR is enabled.
implication: OCR should be opt-in/auto-routed, dependency-checked, timeout-bounded, and not run on every clean page.

claim: PyMuPDF4LLM warns that forced OCR on clean text PDFs slows processing and may reduce output quality; it recommends page-selective OCR and caching.
implication: `cnv` should default to `--ocr auto`, preserve clean text extraction, and cache OCR-derived PDFs.

claim: pdf-inspector exposes text/scanned/mixed classification, confidence, and page numbers needing OCR rather than all-or-nothing OCR.
implication: `cnv` should evolve toward page/document OCR routing signals even if first OCRmyPDF integration is document-level.

claim: The repo wiki recommends detecting scan-heavy pages, creating a searchable derivative PDF locally, then feeding that PDF back into the normal pipeline.
implication: This validates the OCR sidecar design against prior project direction.

claim: The repo wiki lists local OCR preprocessing, tagged-PDF support, numeric-heavy tables, geometry-aware tables, debug artifacts, and encoding-health checks as high-value next improvements.
implication: The plan should sequence OCR with measurable table/tag/debug work, not only add OCR flags.

claim: Current `cnv` processed 44 PDFs under `example/pdf` with 44 successes and 0 failures.
implication: The baseline is stable enough to compare future outputs against conversion success, warnings, images, table markers, headings, and selected fixture content.

claim: The top-level `example/pdf` set contains 22 PDFs; all converted successfully. `golden__chinese_scan.pdf` produced only an image reference and a scan-heavy warning.
implication: Local OCR should target `golden__chinese_scan.pdf` first and must turn image-only output into text-bearing Markdown.

claim: The financial statement baseline has some good GFM table output but still includes glued numeric list rows such as `17) Interessi...3.027.000...`.
implication: Numeric-heavy table repair remains a separate pass from OCR.
